Scenario.title: The SolarWinds Compromise: A Complex Attack on Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex The SolarWinds Compromise was a sophisticated cyber attack campaign targeting various industries, including transportation systems.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Financial gain and intellectual property theft

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Vulnerability, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, SSL Certificates and Attribution, WHOIS records, contacts and history, TCP/IP ports, Public and private cloud, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Application compromise, Vulnerability exploit, Malware, Man in The Middle(MiTM), Session hijacking, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS), Watering-hole, Compromised and weak credentials, Credential compromise, Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS), Replay Attack, Cryptanalysis Attacks, Side Channel Attacks, Hardware Trojans, Covert Attacks, Sleep Deprivation Attack, Clock Skewing, Attacks Targeting System Reputation, Data and Command Integrity Attack, Eavesdropping, Command and Control Attacks, Impersonations/Spoofing, Improper usage, Dumpster diving, Advanced Persistent Threat(APT), Baiting, Shoulder surfing, Reverse social engineering, Business Email Compromise(BEC), Advanced Fee, Extortion

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 538 - Name: Open-Source Library Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers gathered public information about the target organization and its employees to tailor their phishing attacks.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers created a fake website mimicking a legitimate one to trick users into entering sensitive information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-8298 [CWE-807]: Microsoft Office Memory Corruption Vulnerability
stage.description: The attackers sent spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office to gain initial access to the target network.

Scenario.summary: The SolarWinds Compromise demonstrated a complex, multi-stage attack against transportation systems, involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
